codeReview.summary: The fix commit adequately resolves the two prior blocking findings. `generate_stream` and `edit_stream` now start live stream workers immediately after the streaming response headers arrive, and those workers parse SSE chunks incrementally before the HTTP body finishes. Both the eager transcript path and the live worker path now require a terminal `image_generation.completed` / `image_edit.completed` event before reporting success, so `[DONE]`, EOF, or socket close alone no longer count as clean completion. I did not find any remaining blocking runtime defects in the combined original+fix implementation, but there is still one non-blocking regression-coverage gap for `edit_stream` incremental delivery.

issues.description: The refreshed contract suite proves delayed-tail incremental delivery only for `generate_stream`. `edit_stream` now has its own live consumer implementation in `src/resources/images.rs:1039`, but there is no analogous delayed-tail timing/assertion test for the edit path—only terminal-truncation coverage at `tests/images_stream_contract.rs:241`. The runtime change looks correct by symmetry, so this is not blocking, but one half of the fix contract is still inferred rather than explicitly regression-tested.

summary: Pass. The fix addresses the prior scrutiny failures: image SSE delivery is now live instead of buffered, and truncated streams without terminal completed events now fail. The only remaining issue I found is non-blocking: add an explicit delayed-tail incremental regression for `edit_stream` to match the generation proof.
